questions.question: Define a sparse matrix and explain its significance in scientific computing. Provide a numerical example of a 4x4 sparse matrix and its triplet representation.
keyPoints: A sparse matrix is one where the majority of elements are zero
Significance: memory efficiency, computation efficiency, and scalability
Triplet representation stores each non-zero element as (row, column, value)
Header row stores (total_rows, total_columns, total_non_zero_elements)
Example: show a 4x4 matrix with few non-zero elements and its triplet form
Memory advantage: t+1 entries vs rows*columns for full representation

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Simple Transpose and Fast Transpose algorithms for sparse matrices. Provide pseudocode or C code for both algorithms and explain their time complexities.
keyPoints: Simple Transpose: O(n * t) time complexity, scans all columns and non-zero elements
Fast Transpose: O(n + t) time complexity, uses auxiliary arrays rowSize and rowStart
Pseudocode or C code for both algorithms
Comparison of time complexities and memory usage

questions.question: Explain the linked list representation of a sparse matrix. How does it differ from the triplet representation? Provide a numerical example and discuss its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Linked list representation: dynamic memory allocation, each node stores (row, col, value, next)
Differs from triplet representation: dynamic vs static memory allocation
Example: show a 4x4 matrix with few non-zero elements and its linked list form
Advantages: efficient insertion and deletion, disadvantages: slower search and access

questions.question: What are the advantages of using sparse matrices in real-world applications? Provide examples of scientific computing, recommendation systems, network analysis, and machine learning.
keyPoints: Memory efficiency, computation efficiency, and scalability
Examples: scientific computing (finite element methods), recommendation systems (sparse user-item matrices)
Network analysis (sparse adjacency matrices), machine learning (sparse feature vectors)

questions.question: Write a C program to add two sparse matrices using their triplet representations. Explain the algorithm and provide a numerical example.
keyPoints: Define a structure for sparse matrix with row, col, value arrays and non-zero count
Read the two sparse matrices and convert to triplet form
Implement addition algorithm: merge two triplet arrays
Handle cases where elements have the same position
Display original and result matrices
